volumeTitle: Amphitryon. The Comedy of Asses. The Pot of Gold. The Two Bacchises. The Captives
label: The Comedy of Asses (Section 3.3.691 - Section 4.1.746)

sections.label: Section 3.3.691
sections.original: Mi Libane , ocellus aureus , donum decusque amoris ,
sections.translation: My Libanus , golden apple of my eye , the gift and the very grace of love ; there ’ s a dear , whatever you wish , I ’ ll do ; prithee , do give us that money .

sections.label: Section 3.3.693
sections.original: Dic igitur med aniticulam , columbam vel catellum ,
sections.translation: Call me , then , your little duck , dove , or your puppet ; your swallow , jackdaw , little sparrow , your mannikin :

sections.label: Section 3.3.695
sections.original: fac proserpentem bestiam me , duplicem ut habeam linguam ,
sections.translation: make of me the reptile that crawls , so that I may have a double tongue [*] ; enfold me in your arms , and embrace my neck .

sections.label: Section 3.3.697
sections.translation: She , embrace you , villain ?

sections.label: Section 3.3.697b
sections.translation: Really , how undeserving I do seem . You shan ’ t for no purpose have uttered a speech so unseemly against me . By my troth , if indeed you expect to get this money , this day you shall carry myself on your shoulders .

sections.label: Section 3.3.700
sections.translation: What ? I , carry you ?

sections.label: Section 3.3.700b
sections.original: Tun hoc feras argentum áliter a me ?
sections.translation: Otherwise , you shan ’ t get this money from me .

sections.label: Section 3.3.701
sections.original: Perii hercle . si verum quidem et decorum erum vehere servom ,
sections.translation: Heavens , I ’ m undone ! Still , if indeed it is decorous for the master to carry the servant , mount .

sections.label: Section 3.3.702b
sections.translation: In this way are proud people wont to be tamed . Stand still then , just as you were wont to do when formerly a boy [*] . Do you understand what I say ? ( He prepares to get upon the shoulders of ARGYRIPPUS .) Aye — so — move on : I praise you much ; not any horse is there more clever than yourself as a horse .

sections.label: Section 3.3.705
sections.translation: Get on , directly .

sections.label: Section 3.3.705b
sections.original: Ego fecero . hem quid istúc est ? ut tu incedis ?
sections.translation: I ’ ll do so . ( He gets on .) Hallo !— what ’ s the matter ? How are you going ? By my troth , I ’ ll deprive you of your barley then , if you don ’ t amble [*] , lifting up your feet .

sections.label: Section 3.3.707
sections.translation: Prithee , Libanus , there ’ s enough now .

sections.label: Section 3.3.707b
sections.translation: Never this day , by my troth , shall you get anything by entreaty . For now up hill with the spur will I push on my steed . After that , I ’ ll deliver you to the millers , that there you may be tortured as you run [*] .

sections.label: Section 3.3.710
sections.original: asta ut descendam nunciam in proclivi , quamquam nequam es .
sections.translation: Stand still , that I may now at once get down for the hill , although you are but a bad one .

sections.label: Section 3.3.711
sections.original: Quid nunc , amabo ? quoniam , ut est libitum , nos delusistis ,
sections.translation: Well now — since you ’ ve both made fun of me just as you liked , are you going to give the money ?

sections.label: Section 3.3.712b
sections.original: Si quidem mihi statuam et aram statuis
sections.translation: Why , yes , if , indeed , you erect to me a statue and an altar , and then sacrifice an ox to me here as though to a God ; for I am the Divinity Salvation to you .

sections.label: Section 3.3.714
sections.original: Etiam tu , ere , istunc amoves abs te atque † ipse me adgredere
sections.translation: Nay , but , master , do you betake yourself away from him , and do you come to me .

sections.label: Section 3.3.715
sections.original: atque illa , sibi quae hic iusserat , mihi statuis supplicasque ?
sections.translation: And , what he has demanded for himself , will you erect a statue for me , and offer prayers to me ?

sections.label: Section 3.3.716
sections.translation: But what Divinity am I to call you ?

sections.label: Section 3.3.716b
sections.translation: Fortune , and that the Propitious one [*] .

sections.label: Section 3.3.717
sections.translation: You are better than he then .

sections.label: Section 3.3.717b
sections.original: An quid est homini Salute melius ?
sections.translation: Why , is there ever anything better for a man than Salvation ?

sections.label: Section 3.3.718
sections.original: Licet laúdem Fortunam , tamen ut ne Salutem culpem .
sections.translation: Though I praise Fortune , still , not to speak in dispraise of the Divinity Salvation —

sections.label: Section 3.3.719
sections.translation: By the powers , but they are good , both of them .

sections.label: Section 3.3.719b
sections.translation: I shall know it , when they have conferred anything that ’ s good .

sections.label: Section 3.3.720
sections.original: Opta id quod ut cóntingat tibi vis .
sections.translation: Wish for that which you desire to befall you .

sections.label: Section 3.3.720b
sections.translation: What if I do wish it ?

sections.label: Section 3.3.720c
sections.translation: It shall come to pass .

sections.label: Section 3.3.721
sections.original: Opto annum hunc perpetuom mihi huius operas .
sections.translation: I wish for her to be devoted to me alone this whole year round .

sections.label: Section 3.3.721b
sections.translation: You have obtained it .

sections.label: Section 3.3.722
sections.translation: Do you really say so ?

sections.label: Section 3.3.722b
sections.translation: I do say so for certain .

sections.label: Section 3.3.722c
sections.original: Ad me adi vicissim atque experire .
sections.translation: Come to me , in my turn , and make trial : wish ardently for that which you especially desire to happen to you ; it shall be done .

sections.label: Section 3.3.724
sections.original: Quid ego aliud exoptem amplius nisi illud cuius inópiast ,
sections.translation: What other thing could I ardently wish for rather than that of which I am in want ?

sections.label: Section 3.3.725
sections.original: viginti argenti commodas minas , huius quas dem matri .
sections.translation: Oblige me with twenty silver minae to give to her mother .

sections.label: Section 3.3.726
sections.original: Dabuntur , animo sis bono face , exoptata optingent .
sections.translation: They shall be given : take care and be of good courage , your wishes shall be fulfilled .

sections.label: Section 3.3.727
sections.original: Vt consuevere , homines Salus frustratur et Fortuna .
sections.translation: Just as they are wont , Salvation and Fortune are deceiving mortals .

sections.label: Section 3.3.728
sections.original: Ego caput huic argento fui hodie reperiundo .
sections.translation: I this day have been the head in finding this money for you .

sections.label: Section 3.3.729
sections.translation: I have been the foot .

sections.label: Section 3.3.729b
sections.original: Quin nec caput nec pes sermoni apparet .
sections.translation: Why , neither head nor foot of your talking is visible ;

sections.label: Section 3.3.730
sections.original: nec quid dicatis scire nec me cur ludatis possum .
sections.translation: I can understand neither what you mean , nor why you are trifling with me .

sections.label: Section 3.3.731
sections.original: Satis iám delusum censeo . nunc rém ut est eloquamur .
sections.translation: I think that now you ’ ve been teased enough ; now let ’ s disclose the matter as it really stands . Give your attention , Argyrippus , if you please . Your father has ordered us to bring this money to you .

sections.label: Section 3.3.733b
sections.translation: How very à propos and opportunely you have brought it .

sections.label: Section 3.3.734
sections.original: Hic inerunt viginti minae bonae , mala opera partae ;
sections.translation: Here , in this , there will be twenty good minae , obtained by bad means :

sections.label: Section 3.3.735
sections.original: has tibi nos pactis legibus dare iussit .
sections.translation: these , on certain conditions , he bade us give you .

sections.label: Section 3.3.735b
sections.translation: Prithee , what are they ?

sections.label: Section 3.3.736
sections.original: Noctem huius et cénam sibi ut dares .
sections.translation: That you would grant him her favours and an entertainment .

sections.label: Section 3.3.736b
sections.translation: Bid him come , I beg . For him who deserves it right well , we ’ ll do what he wishes , him who has brought these scattered loves of ours to a happy result .

sections.label: Section 3.3.738b
sections.translation: You ’ ll permit your father then , Argyrippus , to caress her ?

sections.label: Section 3.3.739b
sections.translation: She , by being restored to me , will easily cause me to permit it .

sections.label: Section 3.3.740
sections.original: Leonida , curre obsecro , patrem huc orato ut veniat .
sections.translation: Prithee , Leonida , run , and beg my father to come here .

sections.label: Section 3.3.741
sections.translation: He has been in the house some time .

sections.label: Section 3.3.741b
sections.translation: He hasn ’ t come this way , at all events .

sections.label: Section 3.3.741c
sections.translation: He came round that way [*] by the lane , through the garden , lest any one of his friends should see him coming here ; he ’ s afraid that his wife may come to know of it . If your mother knew about the money , how it was obtained —

sections.label: Section 3.3.744b
sections.translation: Well , well —

sections.label: Section 3.3.745
sections.translation: do use words of good omens [*] ;

sections.label: Section 3.3.745b
sections.translation: go in-doors quickly ,

sections.label: Section 3.3.745c
sections.translation: farewell .

sections.label: Section 3.3.745d
sections.translation: And you two , love on .

sections.label: Section 4.1.746
sections.original: Agedum istum ostende quem conscripsti syngraphum
sections.translation: Come now , show me this agreement that you ’ ve written out between myself and the procuress . Read over the conditions ; for you are a quite unique composer [*] in such matters .
